Patch Set 3:

I changed 'op_data = ll_prep_md_op_data(...)' to 'op_data = ERR_PTR(-EINVAL);', and confirmed that we not only need to set stop flag to both thread and agl_thread. Otherwise, either parent thread will block or ll_sai_put will crash.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

Please take the time to test the error path before resubmitting.

Lai, It seems that by clearing lli_opendir_key we are preventing ll_stop_statahead() from dropping the last reference to sai. But it's impossible to really say because nowhere is it stated which references are counted. Does lli_sai count? If so why doesn't ll_stop_statahead() set lli_sai to NULL after calling ll_sai_put(lli->lli_sai)?

There are comments like: "Put the ref which was held when first statahead_enter. It maybe not the last ref for some statahead requests maybe inflight."

References should be clearly associated with pointers and not with control flow.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

(1 comment)

John, you're right. And in http://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/9663 lli_opendir_key is only set by .open and .release, which isolated opendir key setting and sai refcounting, and make statahead logic more straightforward.

parents.subject: LU-5312 fld: retry fld rpc until the import is closed

commit.subject: LU-5274 llite: fix ll_statahead_thread() problems on failure
commit.message:
LU-5274 llite: fix ll_statahead_thread() problems on failure

When ll_prep_md_op_data() fails, ll_statahead_thread() does not
release reference of sai and parent dentry. It does not wake
up parent thread either. This patch fixes these problems.

Signed-off-by: Li Xi <lixi@ddn.com>
Change-Id: Ibcc527d229e8a78710ff2263eb97969c7c46ecdb
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/10940
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Tested-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Lai Siyao <lai.siyao@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
